title: SELECT DISTINCT
content:
### SELECT DISTINCT

The DISTINCT keyword eliminates duplicate rows from the result set. It is used immediately after SELECT.

---

### Basic DISTINCT Syntax

```sql
-- Get all unique departments
SELECT DISTINCT department FROM employees;

-- Get unique combinations of department and location
SELECT DISTINCT department, location FROM employees;
```

### DISTINCT vs ALL

By default, SELECT returns all rows including duplicates. DISTINCT filters out duplicates.

| Clause | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| SELECT ALL department | Returns all department values (default) |
| SELECT DISTINCT department | Returns each department value only once |

### DISTINCT with Multiple Columns

When multiple columns are specified, DISTINCT considers the combination of values across those columns:

```sql
-- Returns unique department/city pairs
SELECT DISTINCT department, city FROM employees;
```

> **Key Point:** DISTINCT applies to all selected columns, not just the first one. NULL values are considered equal for DISTINCT purposes.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of the DISTINCT keyword?
options: To remove duplicate rows from the result set
To filter rows by a condition
To count the number of rows

quiz.question: SELECT DISTINCT department, location considers uniqueness based on:
options: The combination of department and location
Neither, it will return an error
